title: Broaden the grab-navigation override input without reopening the player core
summary: 0.6.29 keeps the 0.6.28 CUA focus and trigger-tap behavior intact, but broadens the temporary grab-navigation override so the official Unity joystick button 5 path can also release CUA gaze capture and restore VaM navigation while the button is held.
changes: Keep the existing thumbstick-click grab-navigation override path in the CUA input module.
Add a narrow Unity joystick button 5 fallback for the same temporary grab-navigation override behavior.
Stamp version 0.6.29 into player.version.json and BuildRuntimeInfo.cs.
Build and raw-deploy the 0.6.29 core-player alpha seam.
reasoning: The current override policy already lives in the right CUA input seam, so this slice stays narrow and avoids reopening playback, sync, playlists, surfaces, or packaging behavior.
The operator reported that the joystick top or 5 control was not actually re-enabling navigation, which points to an input-source mismatch rather than a bad focus policy.
Official VaM-managed input surfaces expose Unity joystick button numbering, so adding the matching button 5 fallback is the smallest safe bridge.
operatorVisibleOutcomes: If the user is looking at the screen and presses the joystick button 5 grab-navigation control, CUA capture releases and navigation is restored while the override is held.
When the override ends, gaze can re-engage naturally only if the user is still looking at the screen and locomotion has stopped.
knownIssues: This slice does not change playback, playlists, slideshow behavior, A-B behavior, volume gestures, or spatial audio.
This slice does not redesign surfaces, panel UI, or TV placement.
